name: Maya Chen
occupation: UX Designer at a mid-size tech company
backstory: Maya moved to Portland after college for a design job she loves. She lives alone in a one-bedroom apartment. After long, stressful days of client revisions and tight deadlines, she opens shopping apps on the couch. The dopamine hit of 'add to cart' is immediate and reliable. She currently has 30+ unopened packages stacked in her hallway closet. She knows it's a problem — she's mentioned it to friends, who laugh it off. She downloaded a meditation app six months ago and uses it maybe once a week.
emotional_triggers: Work stress and feeling undervalued after tough client feedback
Instagram lifestyle content — especially 'apartment tour' and 'desk setup' posts
Flash sales and 'last chance' notifications from shopping apps
Sunday evening dread before the work week
Loneliness on weeknights when friends are busy
existing_strengths: Creative and visually oriented — finds satisfaction in design work
Self-aware enough to recognize the impulse buying pattern
Has a meditation app she occasionally uses
Close relationship with her sister who lives two hours away
Enjoys cooking when she has the energy
current_situation: Maya just got a push notification for a 40% off sale at her favorite home goods store. She has three tabs open with throw pillows, a ceramic vase, and a desk lamp. She hasn't opened the last two packages she ordered. She's feeling the familiar pull and decided to talk to someone about it instead of clicking 'buy.'
what_success_looks_like: Maya closes the tabs, feels okay about it, and maybe does something with her evening that she'll actually remember tomorrow.
